steps.title: Identify what the claim requires
steps.content: The claim states the poem conveys “contrasting feelings” about New York City versus Jamaica. The quotation must illustrate this contrast—showing both places in tension, or showing the speaker's conflicting emotions about them.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B presents the explicit contrast: NYC at dawn (“'Tis dawn, dawn in New York...I watch them sadly shuffle on”) followed immediately by the speaker's yearning (“But I would be on the island of the sea”).

This captures both the present reality and the emotional pull toward Jamaica in a single quotation, directly illustrating the divided feelings.

The other three focus on only one location—A and D on NYC imagery, C on Jamaica—without revealing the emotional tension between them.

That confirms B is correct.
